An information disclosure vulnerability in the Android media framework (libstagefright). Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-63522430.

In CollectValuesOrEntriesImpl of elements.cc, there is possible remote code execution due to type confusion. This could lead to remote escalation of privilege with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android. Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111274046. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because the precheck feature in elements.cc does not check for the right type during a loop. This precheck only checks for the first element of the vector, but then the loop is executed. If in the loop a different type is encountered, then the precheck is bypassed and the type-specific code is executed. The attacker must session_geroot to trigger this vulnerability. Details: https://android.g

The Pointer-Compression implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.4.6, when run in an application that uses the GTK+ graphical library, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted length value in a DTLS (Differentiated Transport Level Security) packet, related to libgcrypt 1.6.4 and the DTLS implementation in OpenSSL 1.9.0 before 1.9.0p. This issue is different than CVE-2012-2783. Note that this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2012-27 fix in libgcrypt 1.7.0.

Use-after-free vulnerability in the wifi_cleanup function in bcmdhd/wifi_hal/wifi_hal.cpp in Wi-Fi in Android 6.x before 2016-02-01 allows attackers to gain privileges by leveraging access to the local physical environment during execution of a crafted application, aka internal bug 25753768. This issue is related to the Wifi HAL. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-34097672. NOTE: this issue also exists in the Android 7.1.1 and 7.1.2 versions of the Wi-Fi HAL. Workaround: None. Android ID: A-3409

The PORCHE_PAIRING_CONFLICT feature in Bluetooth in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 allows remote attackers to bypass intended pairing restrictions via a crafted device, aka internal bug 26551752.

In FT_Stream_EnterFrame of stream.c in FreeType before 2.5.4, a certain error condition was mishandled. If the next byte contains 0xCD and the bytes 0xAB and 0xEE are then found somewhere in the following 64 bytes, a heap-based buffer over-read would occur. This would likely be triggered by a crafted font file. This is similar to CVE-2017-9820. Note that FreeType 2.5.4 was not affected by the original issue, but the replacement string in FT_Stream_UseMagic now includes 64 bytes, which makes it more likely that the heap-based buffer over-read could occur. We doubt that any user has encountered this vulnerability, as the code path that triggers it requires data from a crafted font file.

The LDAP server in the AD domain controller in Samba 4.x before 4.1.22 does not check the client's size limit before attempting to copy bytes into the packet object, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption and daemon crash) via a large request.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2010-0212.  NOTE: this issue was originally reported as CVE-2010-1094, but was mapped to CVE-2010-0212.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2010-0212 by request of the vendor for tracking purposes.  CORE-4235449.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-20

media/libmedia/mediametadataretriever.cpp in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 mishandles cleared service binders, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 26040840.

The I/O implementation for block special files (e.g., /dev/rdx) does not properly handle the case where one I/O call provides more data than requested, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (looping I/O) by triggering an inefficient read operation. This is different from CVE-2013-7090, which affects the ext4 filesystem implementation in the Linux kernel. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2013-7090. The vendors have confirmed that the vulnerability exists in the product as described. (The Product Technical Support Engineer explained that the vulnerability exists because the "original_len" variable is reset to the correct value (i.e., not too small) after the data is copied to the buffer.)** UPDATE: this vulnerability was SPLIT from CVE-20

The virgl_renderer_resource_create function in virgl/resource-create.c in virgl-renderer before 0.6.0 allows local guest OS users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and QEMU process crash) by using an invalid (i.e., too large) resource header. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2018-12044. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2018-12044.

The Linux kernel before 5.1-rc5 allows page->_refcount reference count overflow, with resultant use-after-free issues, if about 140 GiB of RAM exists. This is related to fs/fuse/dev.c, fs/pipe.c, fs/splice.c, include/linux/mm.h, include/linux/pipe_fs_i.h, kernel/trace/trace.c, mm/gup.c, and mm/hugetlb.c. It can occur with FUSE requests. A workaround exists. Refer to issue number 170 (https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=170). NOTE: this bug is related to bug 170 (https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=170).

The ocfs2 filesystem implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.1 does not properly validate extents during writes, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (unspecified) as a side effect of modifying a file. This is related to the extents feature implementation and the fs/ocfs2/cluster/nodemanager.c, fs/ocfs2/ocfs2_dio.c, and fs/ocfs2/ocfs2_file.c modules. Note that this vulnerability exists because the OS is not started with the nodelay feature enabled.

A malicious webview could install long-lived unload handlers that re-use an incognito BrowserContext that is queued for destruction in versions of Oxide before 1.18.3. This allows attackers to launch malicious code in a separate process. (No Android component is affected.) Fixed in 1.18.3. -- Gabor Szabo ( szabgab@google.com ) 2018-08-09 This issue is timely addressed and worth highlighting for any Oxide project users. We had a quick meeting and we decided to release an update to v1.18.2. This update will be available on the Play Store soon. In the meantime, if you use Oxide, please upgrade your apps to target the v1.18.2 or later version. Thank you for your cooperation. Reach us at oxidemedia@googlegroups.co

The DV encoder in libavcodec before 55.71.100, and in libav 11.5 and earlier, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and crash) via crafted DV frames. It is related to libav/dvenc.c, libav/dvps.c, and libav/dvvideo.c. (Because of a missing function, the dvps.c and dvvideo.c files are not used in the current release.) NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-0819. Updates to the dvvideo.c file in the current release do not provide a complete fix for CVE-2016-1523.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792. Read-write memory corruption can occur without a remote attack if a crafted mkv file is played in an Android media player that uses the mkvparser library for MPEG-4 conformance testing. This attack achieves Code Execution privileges in the mkvparser process. Converted to Remote Code Execution as rce_mkvparser.py. Namespaces: mkvparser, mediaserver

In PHP before 5.4.41, 5.5.x before 5.5.24, and 5.6.x before 5.6.8 there is a use after free in the OpenSSL extension on objects that are closed with a reference to an object that was not closed before the change in 5.5.21. This is similar to CVE-2015-3155 but differs in that it only affects objects that are closed with a reference to a previously unclosed object. Because the extension uses a different reference scheme, the use after free effects are not observable using code that triggers the extension. The only way to trigger this is to explicitly assign to the property that was closed, e.g. $x['ssl'] = ... The reference that is dangling is that of a client object. We do not consider this a vulnerability because OpenSSL is not directly visible from PH

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the case of default parser flags, local files can be included with file: protocol, and remote files with HTTP or FTP protocol. If a DTD is referenced, remote DTDs may be loaded (which might be otherwise unreachable). Overflows are possible for entities or attributes that use an invalid UTF-8 sequence. In fs/ext4/extents.c in the Linux kernel before 3.13, there is a possible out-of-bounds read if the inode has a non-standard extent size. This could happen if an application reads a large file where none of the data is present in memory. In this case, the file's data is on disk, and the extents structure is used to look up the appropriate on-disk metadata. If the extents code encounters an unexpected extent size, it will read from the extents structure without checking the boundaries. This could lead to a crash of the kernel. This is possible only if the ext4 filesystem is mounted with the extents option, which may be the case for a shared mount from a system with a buggy kernel. An out-of-bounds memory access issue was found in Quick Emulator (QEMU) before 1.7.2 in the VNC display driver. This flaw could occur while refreshing the VNC display surface area in the 'vnc_refresh_server_surface'. A user inside a guest could use this flaw to crash the QEMU process. Reverse engineered code is available. This issue is similar to CVE-2017-1000145. A user inside a guest could use this flaw to read host memory by providing a large width in a savevm image. This is different from CVE-2017-1000145 (which should be fixed in QEMU before 1.7.1).

In libavcodec in FFmpeg 4.0.1, a missing check for failure of a call to init_get_bits8() in the avcodec_decode_video2() function may trigger a NULL pointer dereference while converting a crafted AVI file to MPEG4, leading to a denial of service or possibly unspecified other impact. This is a rare issue that should affect only users who intentionally convert crafted AVI files to MPEG4. The bug was found while running NSSMALLER and sigcheck. Library versions affected: libavcodec in FFmpeg 4.0.1, libavcore in FFmpeg 4.0.1, and libavfilter in FFmpeg 4.0.1. Reported in https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1436259

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the case of an entity inclusion, the entity is loaded into the calling process's address space, even if not referenced in the parsed XML document. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-1000671. It is recommended to upgrade to at least version 2.9.4 in order to get the full protection in this case. NOTE: this issue is considered to be similar to CVE-201

Irssi before 0.8.15, when SSL is used, does not verify that the server hostname matches a domain name in the subject's Common Name (CN) field or a Subject Alternative Name field of the X.509 certificate, which allows man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof IRC servers via an arbitrary certificate.  NOTE: this is a potential issue only when the --ssl option is used.  It is not clear whether the same approach can be used to spoof an SSL session for an cleartext Irssi client because the server hostname check only occurs after the initial SSL handshake.

Race condition in the IP implementation in the Linux kernel before version 3.0. This race occurs when a certain IP packet matches a certain rule in the iptables configuration, and the rule matches both the packet's source and destination IP addresses. In this case, the iptables rule's action is performed twice and, in the case of a NF_QUEUE action, the queue can grow indefinitely. NOTE: this issue exists because of an errata in the 2.6.31 kernel that is not related to the iptables package. The iptables package does not include the bugfix included in the 2.6.31.12 kernel. The _bsd_recvmsg function implements receive operations for a socket with the MSG_PEEK flag set using the recvmsg and recvfrom system calls. Buffer overflows are possible because the caller can provide a msg_name parameter that points to a buffer too small, which will be overwritten with a random string. The msg_namelen parameter should be set to the size of the buffer in order to avoid this issue, and the code should be updated if a smaller buffer is passed. This is a rare issue, as it is not likely that a msg_name parameter would be pointing to a buffer of size zero. (http://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2016/09/05/17/42) A remote attacker could leverage this vulnerability to cause a buffer overflow and execute arbitrary code. The vulnerability is in the arch/x86/kvm

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. In the default configuration, only files with a total header size of <= 1024 bytes can have this vulnerability. This is because the function calls *malloc()* for each data block in the ID3 frame, and the default setting for *setjmp()* size in malloc.c is 64 KB, which means that the malloc implementation uses a stack-based overflow detection function. A crafted FLAC file can be made with, for example, *fluid-soundflower*. A crafted file can be processed with a program (like *libsndfile* itself) that was built with the default settings for *setjmp()* size

An out-of-bounds memory access issue was found in Quick Emulator (QEMU) before 1.7.2 in the VNC display driver. This flaw could occur while refreshing the VNC display surface area in the 'vnc_refresh_server_surface'. A user inside a guest could use this flaw to crash the QEMU process. Individual steps to reproduce are not known, but a crash could be caused by sending a special nested control message. (Jenkins test framework). We refer the user to the attached patch for a possible workaround. See also bugzilla #6548. Refresh surface in VNC display driver should check surface dimensions Read access length in refreshing surface in VNC display driver.

A higher risk exists if support for IPv6 is enabled in which case both the IPv4 and IPv6 timers are vulnerable. Scan a target host with nmap and use -sV for verification. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2004-0920. A syscall was added in Linux 2.6.28 and therefore this issue is not applicable to that kernel version or later. The LDAP server in the AD domain controller in Samba 4.x before 4.1.22 does not check return values to ensure successful ASN.1 memory allocation, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption and daemon crash) via crafted packets.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-6287. It is different from CVE-2014-6288.

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792. Note that this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect way to access mode_info.

libvpx in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.0 before 2016-03-01 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted media file, related to libwebm/mkvparser.cpp and other files, aka internal bug 23452792
A vulnerability in the Android media framework (n/a). Product: Android. Versions: 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 8.0. Android ID: A-62187433. Android ID: A-62187433. Not publicly documented.

The nr_recvmsg function in net/netrom/af_netrom.c in the Linux kernel before 3.9-rc7 does not initialize a certain data structure, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory via a crafted recvmsg or recvfrom system call. In the meantime, the nr_sendmsg function is also vulnerable. This issue is different from CVE-2011-1017. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2011-1017.

mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-06-01 does not validate OMX buffer sizes, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27207275.

The extract_to function in archive.c in BusyBox before 1.27.2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted zip archive, because it calls unlink without checking the return value. NOTE: although the vulnerability is in extract_to, it affects function main_zip, too, because it calls extract_to. We note this only because the cause of the vulnerability may be different in both functions. We have disclosed this vulnerability to the BusyBox maintainers and they have committed a fix. We have prepared an additional patch, which we will send to them. The fix will be included in the next release of BusyBox

The convert_utf16_to_utf8 function in ef_conf.c in the Enlightenment before 0.17.6 allows local guest OS users to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via unspecified vectors. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-9385. NOTE: this issue is only relevant to Enlightenment implementations that parse UTF-16 strings

The encode_share_access function in fs/nfs/nfs4xdr.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.29 allows local users to cause a denial of service (BUG and system crash) by using the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of a missing lock requirement in the nfs_file_open function in fs/nfs/nfs4.c. This is unrelated to CVE-2011-4131.

media/libmedia/SoundPool.cpp in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49G, and 6.x before 2016-02-01 mishandles locking requirements, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 25781119. npqbgeuiw.bpmcn.ejpwbjl. A remote attacker who can trigger this issue may execute arbitrary code within the context of a mediaserver process. This issue is rated Low due to completion requirements and because it can be triggered very late in a multi-session situation where a malicious application can be used to gain Signature access. Product: Android. Versions: 4

In CollectValuesOrEntriesImpl of elements.cc, there is possible remote code execution due to type confusion. This could lead to remote escalation of privilege with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android. Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111274046. NOTE: this issue exists because the app reads user input and then passes that input to android.util.Pair. The pair will be treated as a Pair<T, T> and the caller will get a Collection<T>. Setting the T type to String will cause the pair to be treated as a Pair<String, String> and the caller will get a Collection<String>. Android ID: A-111274046

GNU wget before 1.18 allows remote servers to write to arbitrary files by redirecting a request from HTTP to a crafted FTP resource. This is similar to the HTTP to FTP vulnerability found in wget 1.16 and 1.17. (Note that wget 1.17 and 1.18 are different versions from the BSD wget.) Example usage: wget -O out.txt http://example.com/index.html ftp://example.com/index.html. To exploit this vulnerability, a user must be logged in when wget is used. Note that this vulnerability exists because wget does not fully sanitize the protocol specification before using it in a call to the opener() function in the function do_protocol(). DBus-GLib 0.73 disregards the access flag of exported GObject properties, which allows local users to bypass intended access restrictions and possibly cause a denial of service by modifying properties, as demonstrated by properties of the (1) DeviceKit-Power, (2) NetworkManager, and (3) ModemManager services. Properties can be modified in two steps: (1) find the D-Bus path of the target object in org.freedesktop.DBus.Properties (e.g., using gdbus), and (2) access the property with the appropriate get and/or set command. NOTE: this issue is identical to CVE-2016-2153 but was missed due to insufficient information. FIXED in 0.74.2 by ensuring that property access is denied if the underlying object does not have the appropriate access flag. * HTTP: HTTP request header

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the case of entity inclusion, this could be exploited to attack other hosts on the network using entity redirection. (For example, in OpenOffice and possibly other XML-based file formats.) A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the case of default parser flags, attackers could read arbitrary files on the system where the attacking XML document is parsed, and possibly execute code on this system if the vulnerable document contained Java or functionality for XML processing. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-1000671. Portions of the attack surface in libxml2 are still available with default parser flags. Fixed in libxml2 2.9

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the case of default parser flags, an attacker could read local files on the system where the vulnerable application is running. If the vulnerable XML content is passed out of the vulnerable application, an attacker could read files on the same system as the vulnerable XML content is displayed, or files on other systems if there is a trust relationship with such a system. Note that this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for the issue identified in ISSUE-ID-20141213-01

The snd_seq_ioctl_remove_events function in sound/seq/seq_clientmgr.c in the Linux kernel before 4.1.3 does not verify FIFO assignment, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and OOPS) via a TIME_QUEUE ioctl call.  NOTE: this issue is also related to *remove_event*.

NOTE: this issue is unique to the crypto functionality in Pacemaker, and does not affect Corosync.  NOTE: while the crypto library must be updated to gain protection from the issue, the application's use of SSL/TLS is still necessary to trigger the issue. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-4973.

The ftp client in the Linux kernel before 3.2.1 allows remote FTP servers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and system crash) via unexpected responses. This affects the ftp_get_reply function in net/ftp.c.

The vfork implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.10.1 does not properly handle situations in which the child process inherits file locks held by the parent process, which allows local users to cause a denial of service (locking) or possibly have unspecified other impact by placing a lock on a named file in the current directory. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because vfork was designed to be unavailable in a future version of Linux. In practice, the use of vfork in general is discouraged.

The ext4 implementation in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-09-01 does not properly handle superblocks with a certain SB_BLOCK_SIZE value, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information from kernel memory by reading the inital contents of a partition, aka internal bug 27147683. This is related to ext4_read_sb_backup in fs/ext4/super.c.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because the Android bug reporting system lacks a mechanism to distinguish between issues that are product-dependent and issues that are customer-dependent. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.

NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-3568.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2014-3568 because it has a different set of affected versions.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-3116.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-380

The TCP stack in the Linux kernel through 4.10.6 mishandles the SCM_TIMESTAMPING_OPT_STATS feature, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from the kernel's internal socket data structures or cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via crafted system calls, related to net/core/skbuff.c and net/socket.c. This is related to a previous vulnerability in the Linux kernel before 4.10.0.

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. In the default configuration, only files with a small number of frames (< 1000) can trigger the bug. No user interaction is needed. Attackers can use this issue to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted FLAC file. The security of a sandboxed environment (e.g., AppSandbox, NSURLIsExemptFromExternalAccessControl) is not affected. (Did not require *configure --enable-debug* for generation.) NOTE: This issue is similar to SEC-1205. Reverse engineered from iTunes 12.7.1.

Use-after-free vulnerability in the DestroyImage function in image.c in ImageMagick before 7.0.6-6 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a crafted file. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-1000180. It is recommended to upgrade to ImageMagick 7.0.6-6.

NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-4375.  NOTE: it is recommended to upgrade to FreeType 2.5.4 to avoid this issue.  NOTE: it is unknown whether the font file contains a kernel table.  If the kernel table is not present, the vulnerability cannot be triggered.  It is assumed that the kernel table is present in the font file, which was created with a crafted tool or by a malicious server. The exif_process_TIFF_in_JPEG function in ext/exif/exif.c in PHP before 5.5.35, 5.6.x before 5.6.21, and 7.x before 7.0.6 does not validate TIFF start data, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) or possibly have unspecified other impact via crafted header data.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2015-4484.

In the PHP implementation of the SOAP actor, a denial of service vulnerability in the SoapServer::handle() method could occur even if the requested method was not marked as being exported for WSDL support. This is due to a missing check in the SoapServer::SoapServer() constructor that sets a flag if the method is marked as being exported for WSDL support. The vulnerability exists because the check is not reached if the requested method is not found in the specified list of exported methods. This issue applies to PHP versions 5.3.2 through 5.3.15 and 5.4.0 through 5.4.13. A remote attacker could leverage this issue to crash the web application or execute arbitrary code. The issue is due to an incomplete fix for ZendBug #55250. This issue only affects PHP with the SOAP extension enabled. This issue i

The tt_face_load_kern function in sfnt/ttkern.c in FreeType before 2.5.4 does not restrict the number of characters in a lookup, which allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via a crafted TrueType font.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2014-2240.  NOTE: it is not known whether the vulnerability exists in the current release version of FreeType before 2.5.4.

Off-by-one error in magick/cache.c in ImageMagick allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (segmentation fault) via unspecified vectors.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-9578.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-5689.  NOTE: this issue was mitigated in ImageMagick 7.0.8-4.

The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. A denial of service vulnerability in libstagefright in Mediaserver could enable an attacker to use a specially crafted file to cause a device hang or reboot. This issue is rated as Moderate because it requires an uncommon device configuration. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. Android ID: A-35763994. This issue is rated as High because it is possible to trigger a denial of service with a single message. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4

A double-free bug in the read_gif function in gifread.c in the GIF lib allows a remote attacker to cause a denial-of-service attack or unspecified other impact via a maliciously crafted file, because last_name is mishandled in a way that causes a mismatch between new_record and last_record.

Dart is not affected by this issue. NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2017-2590 because the latter was erroneously marked as FXFIX and should be fixed by Dart-SDK: DART-69042. NOTE: this issue was

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9. There is a NULL pointer dereference for a pf data structure if alloc_disk fails in drivers/block/paride/pf.c. This affects the PARIDE and PAROBDRV drivers. Criticality is medium because the paride and parobdrv drivers have been removed in Linux 2.6.31 and 3.0, respectively. Use of these drivers in combination with a PF disk can cause a system crash or reboot. ERP is not applicable as the PF data structure is only used in the paride and parobdrv drivers. Product: Linux. Versions: 2.6.32, 2.6.34, 2.6.35, 2.6.36, 2.6.37, 2.6.38, 2.6

The pdf_to_num function in pdf-object.c in MuPDF before 1.10 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (use-after-free and application crash) via a crafted file. The vulnerability occurs because an object stream is mishandled in which the result is that an incorrect end object is matched.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2017-9111.  NOTE: this issue was originally reported as CVE-2017-9111. NOTE: this affects MuPDF before 1.10.0.

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9. There is a NULL pointer dereference for a pf data structure if alloc_disk fails in drivers/block/paride/pf.c. This affects the PARIDE device driver in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9.

The Linux kernel before 5.1-rc5 allows page->_refcount reference count overflow, with resultant use-after-free issues, if about 140 GiB of RAM exists. This is related to fs/fuse/dev.c, fs/pipe.c, fs/splice.c, include/linux/mm.h, include/linux/pipe_fs_i.h, kernel/trace/trace.c, mm/gup.c, and mm/hugetlb.c. It can occur with FUSE requests. A workaround exists. Product: Android. Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111893951. Details: https://android.googlesource.com/kernel/common/+/

The Framework in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-09-01 allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 27833616. Apps that require cross-process access should register their cross-process binders in a different process com.android.internal.util.ee.EE.

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This does not require any privilege elevation.

In Asterisk before 1.11.1, 1.12.x before 1.12.4, and 1.13.x before 1.13.1, an attacker can cause a denial of service (daemon hang or crash) by sending a crafted message that triggers a certain data structure allocation. This is related to channels/abstract-socket.c, channels/socket.c, and cli/streaming.c. A crafted message will trigger a call to app_daemon in apps.c. A vulnerable option in apps.c is marked as CLI_OPTIONS.

Das U-Boot versions 2016.09 through 2019.07-rc4 can memset() too much data while reading a crafted ext4 filesystem, which results in a stack buffer overflow and likely code execution. This occurred at longlink_setup() time. This attack appears to be remote. Memset is a variable instruction in lib/longlong.c. A patch is to be found in the ext4 branch here: https://github.com/random-archer/u-boot/commit/c306a0a834b2310e43840009293707a5b8b8f839b1 In the meantime, disable the ext4fs functionality with "setenv bootargs console=ttymxc0,115200n8". In rfc_process_mx_message of rfc_ts_frames.cc, there is a possible out of bounds read due to a missing bounds check. This could lead to remote information disclosure with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-80432928. Android ID: A-80432928. Android ID: A-111937065.

In Bluetooth stack of Android 4.x.x 5.x.x before 5.1.1 LMY49G and 6.x before 2016-03-01, an untrusted update of a kernel heap object could trigger a use-after-free. This update is performed by the bta_av_proc_meta_data function in drivers/bluetooth/bluez/profiles/audio/audio_bta.c. The vulnerability exists because the avrc_pars_browsing_cmd function in drivers/bluetooth/bluez/profiles/av/browsing/avrc_browsing.c does not check the 'label' parameter. Android products, like Pixel and Pixel XL, are affected. Android ID: A-34970788. Details: https://support.google.com/a/answer/7

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 4.20.15. The nfc_llcp_build_tlv function in net/nfc/llcp_commands.c may return NULL. If the caller does not check for this, it will trigger a NULL pointer dereference. This will cause denial of service. This affects nfc_llcp_build_gb in net/nfc/llcp_core.c. Both functions are used by nfc_llcp_build_dd_fragment in net/nfc/llcp_fragment.c. This affects Android before 7.0 nfc_ndef_util_llcp_build_prop_req in external/nfc_nci/nfc_llcp_util.c. llcp_build_tlv is called by llcp_build_gb in llcp_core.c via llcp_build_d

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). In the worst case, this may lead to remote code execution. To fix this, libxml2 can be built with a new entity loader API, which allows explicit control of entity loaders for each parser context. Users are urged to upgrade to libxml2 2.9.1 or newer, or to manually disable default parser flags by calling xmlParseFile/xmlParseMemory with the flags value of XML_PARSE_NOENT. Full details are available at http://xml.org/a

An issue was discovered in rds_tcp_kill_sock in net/rds/tcp.c in the Linux kernel before 5.0.8. There is a use-after-free if register_netdev() fails to register a netdevice. The problem can occur with rds_connect() calls, even before the first socket has been bound to a transport. A local user authenticated network operator can cause a denial of service, or possibly have unspecified other impact. Only rds users will be affected by this issue because the rds_tcp module must be loaded manually for now. Kernel versions 5.0.12+ will not be affected because the module will be loaded automatically. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incorrect net device registration. The problem is not related to RDS sockets but to a lack of a network subsystem exit call. Other types of sockets or transport types may be at risk. Detail

fs/ext4/extents.c in the Linux kernel through 5.1.2 does not zero out the unused memory region in the extent tree block, which might allow local users to obtain sensitive information by reading uninitialized data in the filesystem. This is related to extents, extents-convert, and extents-multi-root implementations. Use of the multi-root feature may be particularly dangerous, as attackers may be able to conduct attacker-initiated use-after-free attacks (aka a "double free") by leveraging the ability to trigger filesystem operations with non-terminated strings. This issue is different from CVE-2018-10020 because the latter attack could only occur with specific (1) metadata or (2) data extents. NOTE: this issue exists because the 'extent' data type is not being used anywhere in the extents-convert code path. Stabilizatio

In AMediaCodecCryptoInfo_new of NdkMediaCodec.cpp, there is a possible out-of-bounds write due to an integer overflow. This could lead to remote code execution in external apps with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is needed for exploitation. Product: Android Versions: Android-7.0 Android-7.1.1 Android-7.1.2 Android-8.0 Android-8.1 Android-9.0 Android ID: A-111603051. Android ID: A-111603051.

The mincore() implementation in mm/mincore.c in the Linux kernel through 4.19.13 allowed local attackers to observe page cache access patterns of other processes on the same system, potentially allowing sniffing of secret information. (Fixing this affects the output of the fincore program.) Limited remote exploitation may be possible, as demonstrated by latency differences in accessing public files from an Apache HTTP Server. Issue: https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?fmt=plain;id=855007. NOTE: the latest Ubuntu and Debian backports also include a patch for this issue. We do not consider this a security issue at this time. References: https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?fmt=plain;id=855007?#101 https://github.com

The encode_share_access function in fs/nfs/nfs4xdr.c in the Linux kernel before 2.6.29 allows local users to cause a denial of service (BUG and system crash) by using the mknod system call with a pathname on an NFSv4 filesystem.

The Bluetooth protocol stack in the Linux kernel before 3.6 does not properly initialize certain structures, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from kernel stack memory via a crafted application that targets the (1) L2CAP or (2) HCI implementation. The L2CAP implementation in especially vulnerable, because it is used for passing information during the pairing process. The HCI implementation is not commonly used for pairing, however, it can be used for sending raw commands and is commonly used to pass information during the setup of a Bluetooth interface, i.e., when a Bluetooth device is added or a software stack is loaded. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-0056. It is different from CVE-2012-0056. NOTE: this problem was originally reported as "Bluetooth: l2cap: "Controlle

The png_read_row function in libpng before 1.2.4 does not properly initialize the current_row variable, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process stack memory by leveraging an application that uses the PNG library to read a crafted PNG file.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2012-4937.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because libpng did not properly use the row_pointer variable in the png_read_row function, which allows remote attackers to obtain sensitive information from process stack memory by leveraging an application that uses the PNG library to read a specially crafted PNG file.  NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from CVE-2012-4937 because it can be exploited without elevated privileges in a different way, and because the two bugs have different vulnerabl

In the png_read_row function in png.c in libpng before 1.2.8, an integer overflow may occur creating a chance of a heap-based buffer overflow. Remote attackers could use this vulnerability to crash a program that reads PNG data. This issue is now fixed in libpng 1.2.8.  NOTE: this issue only affects Android 4.x before 4.4.4 and 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 because they use older (fixed) versions of libpng. The NFSv4 implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.2.2 does not properly handle bitmap sizes in GETACL replies, which allows remote NFS servers to cause a denial of service (OOPS) by sending an excessive number of bitmap words.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4131.  NOTE: this issue is only exploitable by NFS servers because the Linux kernel 3.2.2 change does not properly handle OPEN replies.  CVE-2012-2236 is expected to be merged soon after this issue is fixed.

In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This occurs because *ids_read()* does not check for padding bytes before reading the *header.tag_size* field. A crafted FLAC file can be created using the *flac* encoder (--loose-id3-padding) or *metaflac* (--remove-id3-padding). This only affects *libsndfile* 1.0.27 and 1.0.28. The Framework UI permission-dialog implementation in Android 6.x before 2016-06-01 allows attackers to conduct tapjacking attacks and access arbitrary private-storage files by creating a partially overlapping window, aka internal bug 26677796. This issue is rated as Moderate because it requires an attacker to trigger the issue in a specific way. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1. Android ID: A-34097672. NOTE: this issue exists because of an incomplete fix for A-33552736. Also, this issue exists in Android 7.1.1 because the permission-dialog implementation has an extra check to prevent tapjacking, which is not relevant for a partially overlapping window.

The TCP stack in the Linux kernel through 4.10.6 mishandles the SCM_TIMESTAMPING_OPT_STATS feature, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from the kernel's internal socket data structures or cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via crafted system calls, related to net/core/skbuff.c and net/socket.c. This is related to the commit that added support for PACKET_TIMESTAMP on June 25, 2017. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2016-9976. **This is a vulnerability of the kernel and not of Android.** Android is not affected. Product: Android. Versions: Android 7.0, Android 7.1.1, Android 7.1.2, Android 8.0, Androi

The PDF parser in pdf.c in PyPDF 1.18 and earlier allows attackers to cause a denial of service (NULL pointer dereference and crash) via a crafted PDF file. The problem was fixed in PyPDF 1.19.0
